title: soap_pre_request
html:
soap_pre_request

soap_pre_request

Receives the SOAP envelope just before Uniface would normally process it, and returns either an amended SOAP envelope that Uniface processes, or returns an error message that will be encapsulated in a SOAP Fault element and sent as a reply to the Web server.

#include <wsciapi.h>

int soap_pre_request(char *vars, unsigned char *in, unsigned char **out, int *len, void **context)

Parameters

  • char *vars—null-terminated string in the UTF-8 character set. It is an unnamed Uniface list that contains a named list (SERVERVARIABLES) of the server variables supported by the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD). This named list contains information gathered in the Web server about the request. For more information, see Server Variables for Pre- and Post-Requests.

  • char *in—pointer to the Web service Request SOAP envelope as received by the Web server and passed to the Uniface Server. At this stage, Uniface has not processed the SOAP envelope. This envelope is expected to be in either UTF-8 or UTF-16 character sets. The length of the data is available in the len parameter.

  • unsigned char **out—address of a pointer that will receive one of the following:

    • Pointer to an amended SOAP envelope—if the SOAP envelope is amended, its length should be set in the len parameter. If an amended SOAP envelope is returned, it is assumed that the buffer is dynamically allocated, and, if the implementation provides a soap_rel_buf() function, it will be called when the envelope is no longer needed.

      The amended SOAP envelope should be in UTF-8 or UTF-16 character sets. Uniface will continue processing as if it had received the amended SOAP envelope from the Web server.

    • Error message—for an Error return status, a null-terminated error message that is packaged into a SOAP Fault response and returned to the client application. In this case, the buffer is assumed not to be dynamically allocated and soap_rel_buf() is not called. This allows allocation errors to be reported.

    An implementation does not need to amend the SOAP envelope, in which case the pointer should be set to NULL.

  • int *len—pointer to an integer. Initially the integer contains the length of the in parameter, and an implementation should set the integer to the length of the amended SOAP envelope in the out parameter.

  • void **context—The implementation may allocate memory for itself to use and should place the address of that memory in this pointer. The pointer will be delivered to the implementation on every call. In this way, the implementation can store data from one call to the next. When Uniface is exiting, a call to soap_rel_buf() will be made to free this memory.

Return Values

The function receives a Uniface list consisting of Web server variables and the Web service request in the form of a SOAP envelope.

  • 0—success. The function can return an amended SOAP envelope that Uniface can proceed to use. If a new envelope is not returned, then the original one is used.

  • 1—error. The implementation can return a null-terminated error message. This error message is packaged into a SOAP Fault by Uniface and returned to the caller.

Related Links
  • SOAP Faults

    SOAP messages are XML-based, and the SOAP protocol defines a SOAP Fault element for returning remote errors to the caller.
